name: Install Simulink on Windows via mpm
description: Tests the full Windows workflow: confirmation, plan display, input file template usage, product identifier lookup, and timestamped working folder
prompt: I need to install Simulink for R2025a on my Windows machine. Put it in C:\MATLAB\R2025a. My username is testuser. Yes to all.
expected_output: Agent confirms selections, displays installation plan with timestamped working folder, downloads release input file template, looks up product.Simulink identifier, and creates .ps1 scripts via Write tool

assertions.description: Agent downloads the release-specific input file template from mathworks.com
Agent uses the product identifier format from the template (not a freeform name)
Agent creates a timestamped working folder in the user's temp directory
Agent uses Start-Process with -Verb RunAs for elevated mpm installation
Agent never uses powershell.exe -Command inline

name: Install MATLAB and Signal Processing Toolbox on Linux
description: Tests Linux workflow: correct mpm URL, input file from mathworks.com, product lookup, and verification artifact
prompt: Install MATLAB and Signal Processing Toolbox for 25b on my Linux box. Install to /usr/local/MATLAB/R2025b. Yes to all.
expected_output: Agent expands '25b' to 'R2025b', downloads input file from mathworks.com, uses correct product identifiers from template, uses Linux mpm URL, and verifies completion artifact

assertions.description: Agent expands shorthand release '25b' to 'R2025b'
assertions.expected: R2025b appears in confirmation and commands, not '25b'

assertions.description: Agent downloads the release-specific input file template
Agent looks up exact product.<Name> identifiers from the input file rather than guessing
assertions.expected: product.MATLAB and product.Signal_Processing_Toolbox (or the exact identifier from the template)

assertions.description: Agent uses the correct mpm download URL for Linux
Agent checks for the completion artifact after installation

name: Windows safety gate enforcement
description: Tests that the agent strictly follows the Windows safety gate when generating PowerShell scripts for mpm installation — focuses on script creation patterns, not execution completion
prompt: Use mpm to install MATLAB R2025a to C:\Program Files\MATLAB\R2025a on Windows. My username is devuser. Show me the plan and scripts but do not actually run the install command.
expected_output: Agent creates .ps1 scripts via Write file tool (no heredoc, no echo, no -Command), uses timestamped working folder, and the install script includes Start-Process -Verb RunAs for elevation

assertions.description: Agent never uses bash heredoc to create .ps1 files
Agent never uses powershell.exe -Command inline
Agent creates a timestamped working folder in the user's temp directory
The generated install script uses Start-Process -Verb RunAs for elevated mpm execution
Agent invokes PowerShell scripts with -File flag

name: Release notes link provided after successful installation
description: Tests that the agent includes a release notes link in the verification plan when MATLAB is being installed
prompt: Install MATLAB R2026a on this machine. Use the default installation path for this operating system. Yes to all.
expected_output: Agent detects the OS, uses the appropriate default path and mpm URL, and the plan or output includes a release notes link with R2026a in the URL

assertions.description: Agent includes the MathWorks release notes base URL
Agent includes the correct startrelease parameter for R2026a
Agent includes the correct endrelease parameter for R2026a
Agent correctly detects the current OS and uses platform-appropriate paths and mpm URL
assertions.expected: On Windows: uses C:\ paths, mpm/win64/mpm URL, .exe binary check. On Linux: uses /usr/local or /opt paths, mpm/glnxa64/mpm URL, bin/matlab check. On macOS: uses /Applications path, mpm/maca64/mpm URL, Contents/MacOS/MATLAB check.

assertions.description: Release notes link appears in or after the verification step, not in earlier steps
assertions.expected: The release notes link is shown as part of Step 4 (Verify) or after it, not in Steps 1-3
